response_json.overview: The strongest opportunities are existing-page updates tied to clear player jobs and supported by multiple signal types, especially the Gift Center and HQ/progression checks. The GSC-driven codes page update is high value but carries the highest duplication and scope risk, so it needs careful human review. Several external-search ideas are useful as discovery signals, but they are not ready to advance because they rely on single-source or unverified claims and could blur cluster roles.

selected_opportunities.rationale: This is the clearest on-site opportunity: strong GSC signals show the codes page already ranks and gets significant impressions, while the low CTR queries suggest a query-page fit issue rather than a need for new content. It matches an existing cornerstone guide and a known backlog history, so human review should focus on first-screen usefulness and intent alignment without crossing canonical claim boundaries.
selected_opportunities.player_value: Better query match for players searching for Gift Center, redeem codes, and UID help, which can improve findability and reduce confusion on an already important page.
selected_opportunities.duplication_risk: Medium to high because the query intent may already be served by another canonical page and any rewrite could blur cluster role separation.
selected_opportunities.next_step: Have the owner review whether the page can be improved within current scope while preserving canonical claims and cluster separation.
claims_to_verify: Whether the query intent is best served by codes.html or another canonical page
Whether any page-level changes can be made without altering protected canonical claims
Whether the improvement stays within approved cornerstone scope

selected_opportunities.rationale: This is a useful cross-validation opportunity for HQ progression guidance, but it should only proceed if the external reference can be verified against canonical memory and at least one additional reliable source. The player job is distinct enough to justify human review, and it fits the existing Progression cluster without implying a new page.
selected_opportunities.player_value: Helps players understand HQ requirements, construction dependencies, and progression planning with fewer mistakes.
selected_opportunities.duplication_risk: Medium because it could duplicate existing HQ guidance if the cross-check does not add a distinct planning job.
selected_opportunities.next_step: Verify the external claims against canonical sources and confirm whether hq.html needs a scoped update or only a note for follow-up.
claims_to_verify: Whether the source adds new information beyond existing canonical coverage

selected_opportunities.rationale: This is a strong candidate for human review because research cost and branch coverage drift are practical player pain points, and the topic aligns with an existing Research page instead of suggesting a new structure. However, the external source is only a discovery signal, so the claims need independent verification before any content work is considered.
selected_opportunities.player_value: Reduces planning errors by helping players avoid outdated research costs, missing branches, and naming conflicts.
selected_opportunities.duplication_risk: Medium because it may overlap with existing research tables or branch coverage pages if the verified scope is narrow.
selected_opportunities.next_step: Cross-check the research cost and branch data against canonical memory and a second reliable source before deciding whether research-costs.html needs an update.

rejected_or_monitor.reason: Useful as a validation signal, but it depends on a single external source and does not yet prove a distinct player job beyond existing Gift Center coverage.
rejected_or_monitor.future_trigger: Revisit if official routing or UID flow is confirmed by a second reliable source or owner confirmation.

rejected_or_monitor.reason: Monitor only. The event claim is derived from an external search result and is not yet verified enough to support a page update.
rejected_or_monitor.future_trigger: Revisit when event mechanics, theme rotation, or rewards are confirmed by canonical memory plus a second source.

rejected_or_monitor.reason: Monitor only. This is a general hero index signal and may duplicate existing hero coverage without adding a distinct player job.
rejected_or_monitor.future_trigger: Revisit if a verified gap appears in hero class coverage, growth mechanics, or roster navigation.

rejected_or_monitor.reason: Monitor only. The source looks like a broad hero guide and could easily copy competitor framing or overlap with existing hero pages without new value.
rejected_or_monitor.future_trigger: Revisit if a verified hero roster or stats gap is identified and can be covered without copying wording.

rejected_or_monitor.reason: Monitor only. Research tables can be high-risk for copy and drift, and this source is not enough to justify an update by itself.
rejected_or_monitor.future_trigger: Revisit if laboratory badge costs are confirmed against canonical data and an owner approves the scope.

global_risks: Single external sources are not sufficient proof for public mechanics, costs, rewards, seasons, or event claims.
Several proposals are high risk for duplication because they map to existing cornerstone pages and may blur cluster roles.
External search results are discovery signals only and must not be used as copy sources.
Analytics signals can indicate opportunity, but they do not prove that a rewrite is needed.
Monitor-only and rejected topics must not advance into the content proposal workflow without new verification.
next_actions: Have an owner review the codes page opportunity for scope-safe updates that preserve protected canonical claims.
Verify the HQ and research cross-check topics against canonical memory plus at least one additional reliable source.
Confirm whether any proposed page changes can be expressed without creating duplicate intent or role overlap.
Keep all external-search ideas in monitoring until their claims are independently validated.
Do not advance monitor-only or rejected topics into editor, reviewer, intake, or run-plan stages.

safety: No content, backlog, manifest, or production files were modified by the LLM adapter.
